Name: MySQL / MariaDB Default Credentials (MySQL Protocol)
description: It was possible to login into the remote MySQL using default credentials.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 95%
detection_result: It was possible to login as user "root" with password "root".
detection_method: Details: MySQL / MariaDB Default Credentials (MySQL Protocol)
Version used: 2025-09-09T05:38:49Z
product_detection_result: Method: MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol)
impact: The following products are know to use such weak credentials:
- CVE-2001-0645: Symantec/AXENT NetProwler 3.5.x
- CVE-2002-1809: Windows binary release of MySQL 3.23.2 through 3.23.52
- CVE-2004-1532: AppServ 2.5.x and earlier
- CVE-2004-2357: Proofpoint Protection Server
- CVE-2006-1451: MySQL Manager in Apple Mac OS X 10.3.9 and 10.4.6
- CVE-2007-2554: Associated Press (AP) Newspower 4.0.1 and earlier
- CVE-2007-6081: AdventNet EventLog Analyzer build 4030
- CVE-2009-0919: XAMPP
- CVE-2014-3419: Infoblox NetMRI before 6.8.5
- CVE-2015-4669: Xsuite 2.x
- CVE-2016-6531, CVE-2018-15719: Open Dental before version 18.4
- CVE-2024-22901: Vinchin Backup & Recovery 7.2 and prior
Other products might be aected as well.
solution: - Change the password as soon as possible
- Contact the vendor for other possible xes / updates

Name: Oracle MySQL Denial Of Service Vulnerability (Feb 2017) - Linux
description: Oracle MySQL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
product_detection_result: Detected by MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.
impact: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability will allow attackers to cause crash of applications
using that MySQL client.
solution: Upgrade to Oracle MySQL version 5.6.21 or 5.7.5 or later.
insight: Multiple errors exist as,
- In sql-common/client.c script 'mysql_prune_stmt_list' function, the for loop adds elements
to pruned_list without removing it from the existing list.

description: Oracle MySQL Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.

Details: Oracle MySQL Server <= 5.6.42 / 5.7 <= 5.7.24 / 8.0 <= 8.0.13 Security Update (.
Version used: 2025-09-09T05:38:49Z
product_detection_result: Method: MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol)
impact: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability can result in unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all MySQL Server accessible data and unauthorized ability to cause a hang or frequently repeatable crash (complete DOS) of MySQL Server.
solution: Updates are available. Apply the necessary patch from the referenced link.
Oracle MySQL Server versions 5.6.42 and prior, 5.7 through 5.7.24 and 8.0 through 8.0.13.
insight: The attacks range in variety and diculty. Most of them allow an attacker with network access via multiple protocols to compromise the MySQL Server.
For further information refer to the ocial advisory via the referenced link.

Details: Oracle MySQL Backronym Vulnerability (Jun 2016) - Linux
Version used: 2025-09-09T05:38:49Z
product_detection_result: Method: MariaDB / Oracle MySQL Detection (MySQL Protocol)
impact: Successful exploitation will allow man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof servers via a cleartext-downgrade attack.
solution: Upgrade to version Oracle MySQL Server 5.7.3 or later.
insight: The aw exists due to improper validation of MySQL client library when establishing a secure connection to a MySQL server using the ssl option.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'

Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled. The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan. Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE. Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: Database Open Access Information Disclosure Vulnerability
description: Various Database server might be prone to an information disclosure vulnerability if accessible to remote systems.
detection_result: Oracle MySQL can be accessed by remote attackers
impact: Successful exploitation could allow an attacker to obtain sensitive information from the database.
solution: Restrict database access to remote systems. Please see the manual of the aected database server for more information.
insight: The remote database server is not restricting direct access from remote systems.
log_method: Checks the result of various database server detections and evaluates their results.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
detection_result: A MySQL server is running on this port
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.25):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.
